mechanism: Persistent high fiscal deficits (~6-7% GDP) sustain risk-on regimes via Kalecki profit channel by maintaining corporate earnings floor; consolidation >2pp erodes income floor and accelerates regime transition toward risk-off
regime_dependence: Relationship is strongest when fiscal deficit is the marginal driver of corporate profits; weakens if private sector credit growth or external demand dominates fiscal impulse

mechanism: Extreme positioning determines the severity and speed of regime transitions — elevated positioning converts ordinary triggers into cascades by amplifying forced selling, margin calls, and liquidity withdrawal dynamics
regime_dependence: Most relevant during transitions from complacent carry to stress regimes; less relevant during gradual normalization or early recovery phases

mechanism: Maturity wall forces refinancing at higher coupons, stressing CLO equity tranches and potentially triggering overcollateralization test failures, which in turn force CLO deleveraging and create a negative feedback loop
regime_dependence: Relationship intensifies as maturities approach and base rates remain elevated; weakens significantly if rates decline 200bp+ or if maturity extensions are widely available

mechanism: Opacity in private credit masks true risk levels, enabling greater positioning buildup than observable indicators suggest, thereby amplifying eventual cascade severity when true conditions are revealed
regime_dependence: Directionally persistent across all regimes but magnitude impact is greatest during late-cycle complacent carry when opacity allows maximum divergence between perceived and actual positioning

mechanism: Basis trade unwind forces Treasury selling at scale, disrupting the risk-free rate benchmark and propagating stress across all asset classes simultaneously, catalyzing cross-asset correlation regime shifts
regime_dependence: Only activates during acute stress when basis trade positions are forcibly unwound; dormant during normal market conditions. March 2020 demonstrated the transmission channel

mechanism: Kalecki-sustained profits mask Minsky fragility accumulation — strong deficit-driven earnings provide cover for increasing leverage and deteriorating credit quality, delaying but ultimately amplifying the eventual Minsky moment
regime_dependence: Most operative when fiscal deficit is the primary profit driver; weakens when organic private sector demand growth dominates corporate earnings

mechanism: CLO structures channel credit into increasingly opaque private credit vehicles, compounding the measurement opacity problem while creating correlated exposures across ostensibly diversified institutional portfolios
regime_dependence: Relationship strengthens during credit expansion phases when CLO issuance is high and documentation standards loosen; weakens during credit contraction when new issuance slows

mechanism: Persistent MOVE-VIX divergence may signal an unstable cross-asset volatility regime; convergence would likely coincide with and amplify a broader correlation regime shift across rates and equity markets
regime_dependence: Interpretation is itself regime-dependent — if structural shift, divergence is a new normal and this relationship is weak; if cyclical misalignment, convergence becomes a catalyst for correlation shift

mechanism: Fed standing repo facilities and emergency lending programs are the primary circuit breakers for basis trade fragility, but their effectiveness is untested during simultaneous high inflation, creating conditional dependency on an unproven backstop
regime_dependence: Relationship only becomes load-bearing during acute stress events requiring Fed intervention; irrelevant during normal market functioning when basis trade operates within risk limits
